0.text: Now I, Mormon, am about to hand over the record I have been making to my son Moroni. I have witnessed almost the entire destruction of my people, the Nephites.

It is many hundreds of years after the coming of Christ that I hand these records to my son. I believe he will witness the complete destruction of my people. May God grant that he survives them so he may write something about them and something about Christ — perhaps someday it will benefit them.

Now, I will explain what I have written. After I made a summary from the plates of Nephi down to the reign of King Benjamin, whom Amaleki mentioned, I searched among the records that had been given to me. I found these plates, which contained this small account of the prophets from Jacob down to the reign of King Benjamin, and also many of the words of Nephi.

The things on these plates pleased me because of the prophecies about the coming of Christ. My ancestors knew that many of those prophecies have been fulfilled. I also know that everything prophesied about us up to this day has been fulfilled, and everything beyond this day must surely come to pass.
Therefore, I chose these things to finish my record on them. The rest of my record I will take from the plates of Nephi. I cannot write even a hundredth part of the things of my people.
I will take these plates, which contain these prophesyings and revelations, and put them with the rest of my record. They are precious to me, and I know they will be precious to my people.

I do this for a wise purpose, for the Spirit of the Lord whispers to me to do so. I do not know all things, but the Lord knows all things that are to come. Therefore, he works in me to do according to his will.
My prayer to God is for my people — that they may once again come to the knowledge of God, the redemption of Christ, and once again be a delightful people.
Now I, Mormon, proceed to finish my record, which I take from the plates of Nephi. I write it according to the knowledge and understanding God has given me.

After Amaleki gave these plates to King Benjamin, Benjamin took them and put them with the other plates containing records handed down by the kings from generation to generation until the days of King Benjamin.

They were handed down from King Benjamin from generation to generation until they came into my hands. I, Mormon, pray to God that they will be preserved from this time forward. I know they will be, because great things are written on them. My people and their relatives will be judged from them at the great and last day, according to the word of God that is written.
Now, about King Benjamin — he had some conflicts among his own people.

The armies of the Lamanites came down from the land of Nephi to battle against his people. But King Benjamin gathered his armies and stood against them. He fought with the strength of his own arm, with the sword of Laban.
In the strength of the Lord, they fought against their enemies until they had killed many thousands of Lamanites. They fought until they drove the Lamanites out of all their lands of inheritance.
After there had been false Christs and they were silenced and punished according to their crimes,

and after there had been false prophets, false preachers, and false teachers among the people — all punished according to their crimes — and after there had been much conflict and many people leaving to join the Lamanites — King Benjamin, with the help of the holy prophets among his people —

for King Benjamin was a holy man, and he ruled over his people in righteousness. There were many holy men in the land, and they spoke the word of God with power and authority. They used much directness because of the stubbornness of the people —
with their help, King Benjamin, by working with all the might of his body and the full strength of his soul, and with the prophets, once again established peace in the land.
